verdict.summary: Guide has the right practical direction on AIMA/MI/fees, but it misidentifies the core 2025 migration law and overstates several legal effects, especially around EU citizens and family reunification.

issues.where: title, tldr, steps[0].title, steps[0].content[0].text, steps[4].content[0].text, sources[2]
issues.current: «Lei 38/2025 — рамочный миграционный пакет» / «Diário da República — Lei 38/2025, Lei de Bases da Migração»
issues.problem: Wrong law number. The 2025 Lei de Bases da Migração is Lei 61/2025, not Lei 38/2025. This makes the title, TLDR, tax section attribution and source materially unreliable.
issues.suggestion: Replace all references to «Lei 38/2025» with «Lei 61/2025» where referring to the Lei de Bases da Migração, and update the DRE source accordingly. Example: «Lei 61/2025 — Lei de Bases da Migração, em vigor desde 23.10.2025».

issues.problem: The cited URL points to Lei 38/2025, not the migration framework law discussed in the guide. Even if the page exists, it is the wrong legal reference for the article’s subject.
issues.suggestion: Replace with the Diário da República page for Lei 61/2025, preferably using the current diariodarepublica.pt URL format and the consolidated version if available.

issues.current: «сводит режимы иностранцев и граждан ЕС ближе к единой системе» / «меняет, как читаются и применяются Lei 23/2007 ... и Lei 37/2006»
issues.problem: Misleading. Third-country nationals under Lei 23/2007 and EU/EEA/Swiss citizens under Lei 37/2006 remain legally distinct regimes. EU free movement rights cannot be treated as being merged into a general migration system; CRUE is still a municipal registration, not an AIMA residence permit.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite more narrowly: «Lei 23/2007 остаётся отдельным режимом для граждан третьих стран; Lei 37/2006 для граждан ЕС/ЕЭЗ/Швейцарии не исчезла. На практике органы могут чаще сверять адрес, налоговую и семейную информацию, но правовые основания остаются разными».

issues.current: «Если семья присоединяется позже, у основного заявителя должны быть действующий ВНЖ, жильё и доход.» / «ВНЖ или виза основного заявителя, подтверждение записи/процесса AIMA, если применимо.»
issues.problem: Too loose for family reunification. A mere visa, appointment or pending AIMA process is not generally enough for a reagrupamento familiar application inside Portugal; in many cases the sponsor must already hold a valid residence permit, and the 2025 reform added stricter timing/eligibility conditions and exceptions depending on the family member category.
issues.suggestion: Separate two situations: «accompanying family member visa at the consulate» versus «reagrupamento familiar after the sponsor is resident in Portugal». Add that for later reunification the sponsor normally needs a valid residence permit and must satisfy the current Lei 23/2007 requirements, including any post-2025 waiting-period or exception rules applicable to the specific family category.

issues.current: «Реформа усиливает принцип...» / «Закон может давать право на reagrupamento familiar, но отсутствие слота...»
issues.problem: The family reunification section is too generic for a 2026 guide. It does not spell out the important post-reform caveat that rules differ by family category and that some applicants may be subject to additional timing/eligibility restrictions, while others may fall under exceptions.
issues.suggestion: Add a clear caveat: «После изменений 2025 года reagrupamento familiar нельзя описывать одной формулой: супруг/партнёр, несовершеннолетние дети, совершеннолетние зависимые дети и родители могут иметь разные условия и исключения. Перед подачей проверяйте актуальную редакцию arts. 98–108 Lei 23/2007 и инструкцию AIMA для вашей категории».

issues.problem: Wrong attribution because the guide names the wrong law. Also the statement is too unsourced for a sensitive tax topic: a reader needs the exact legal basis and current AT guidance, not only a migration-law reference.
issues.suggestion: Replace with the correct law reference and add the Autoridade Tributária / Código do IRS / implementing rules source. If the regime is not fully operational or depends on implementing regulation, say so explicitly: «проверяется по AT и подзаконным правилам; не является иммиграционным статусом».

issues.current: «Для учёбы — студенческая виза...» / route list omits D4/D5/D6/D3 mapping
issues.problem: The guide repeatedly says D1-D8 but does not map the codes. This is confusing for readers because D3, D4, D5, D6, D7 and D8 have distinct uses; family is usually D6/temporary-stay/accompanying-family route depending on facts, not just a generic «family» route.
issues.suggestion: Add a compact visa-code box: D1 employed work; D2 independent/professional/entrepreneurial activity; D3 highly qualified activity; D4 study/research/volunteering depending on subcategory; D5 student mobility; D6 family reunification/accompanying family; D7 passive income/pension; D8 remote work/digital nomad.

issues.current: «CRUE ... в быту он нужен для банка, SNS, школы, обмена водительских прав и налоговой резидентности.»
issues.problem: Overstated. CRUE is legally required after 3 months of residence for EU citizens, but it is not always strictly required for every listed service, and tax residency follows Código do IRS criteria rather than CRUE itself.
issues.suggestion: Write: «CRUE часто просят или он сильно упрощает банк, SNS, школу и обмен прав, но налоговая резидентность определяется правилами IRS, а не самим CRUE».

issues.current: «В 2026 ожидайте больше сверок между Câmara, Finanças, Segurança Social и AIMA...»
issues.problem: Speculative and unsourced. It may be a fair practical warning, but as written it sounds like an established legal mechanism.
issues.suggestion: Soften wording: «На практике возможны сверки и запросы дополнительных доказательств, поэтому держите адрес, работу, IRS и семейные документы согласованными».

issues.current: «133 € за приём/анализ заявления и 307,20 € за выдачу ВНЖ по art. 75 Lei 23/2007»
issues.problem: The amounts appear consistent with the 01.03.2026 AIMA fee update, but the guide cites only the generic AIMA homepage. For fees, a generic homepage is not enough; readers need the fee table/legal act because AIMA fees vary by procedure and urgency/duplicates/renewals.
issues.suggestion: Keep the amounts, but add the exact source for the fee table and phrase as procedure-specific: «для указанной процедуры по tabela de taxas AIMA vigente desde 01.03.2026; другие процедуры/renovação/2.ª via могут стоить иначе».
issues.source: AIMA tabela de taxas vigente desde 01.03.2026; Portaria 480-A/2025/1

issues.current: Sources include Lei 23/2007, Lei 37/2006, wrong Lei 38/2025 and AIMA homepage only.
issues.problem: Missing primary sources for several factual claims: abolition of manifestação de interesse, 2026 SMN/IAS, IRS rate, IFICI/IRS-Migrante and AIMA fees.
issues.suggestion: Add separate sources for: Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024 or the consolidated Lei 23/2007 provisions ending the old manifestação de interesse route; 2026 SMN legal act; 2026 IAS legal act; Código do IRS/AT tables for the 11.97% bracket; IFICI legal basis and AT guidance; AIMA fee table effective 01.03.2026.
issues.source: Decreto-Lei 37-A/2024; Código do IRS; Autoridade Tributária; AIMA

missing_topics: Correct identification of Lei 61/2025 as the Lei de Bases da Migração and its entry into force on 23.10.2025
Explicit transitional rules for people who had manifestação de interesse or pending processes before the 2024 abolition
Post-2025 family reunification restrictions, exceptions and category-specific rules
Exact official source for AIMA fees effective 01.03.2026
Official tax sources for IRS-Migrante/IFICI, SMN 2026, IAS 2026 and the 2026 IRS brackets
